description: This figure contains two correlated parts: the left part is an optical micrograph of the pre-CMP treatment measurement region on SPCC/SPCE low carbon steel, presenting clear grain boundary morphologies of the alloy's metallographic structure. The right part is a schematic diagram of the nanoindentation test point grid, with coordinate axes in unit of μm covering a 3 μm × 2 μm area, where each hollow circle denotes a measured point for micro-scale elastic modulus detection. This figure specifies the test region for subsequent nanoindentation characterization, which aims to distinguish mechanical property differences between grain interiors and grain boundaries. The related test results support the core conclusion that dislocation pile-up near grain boundaries after plastic deformation reduces the apparent elastic modulus, providing microscopic evidence for optimizing stamping spring-back FEM simulation accuracy.
